title: Idempotent Consumers, Inbox Records, and External Effects · Inference Brief: External Effect Dedup
summary: A consumer uses an in-memory set, marks messages complete before effects, reuses IDs across tenants, and assumes an idempotency key makes non-transactional email and payment calls exactly once. Planned artifact: Add and verify the external effect dedup increment in durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use provider idempotency, effect ledgers, reconciliation, or compensation for non-transactional external effects and state residual ambiguity.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict External Effect Dedup · Theory External Effect Dedup
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6772 in a consumer fleet under a shared dependency outage. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
